name: Pagination
summary: Tab-style page navigator with an elastic active-page underline; controlled by `count` + `page` + `onPageChange` — use it to page through large lists or tables.

bestPractices: Use `onPageChange` to receive the new 1-based page (`page` is controlled).

Keep visible pages small. For large `count`, set `visibleWindow` (with `autoFollowActive`, default true) so only a window of page buttons shows plus window-scroll controls; rely on the prev/next controls for stepping.
Targets are touch-friendly automatically — on coarse pointers each nav button floors at ≥44px tall (24px under `compact`); pick spacing/density rather than hand-sizing for mobile.
Sync state when relevant. Mirror page in the router (query or path) so refresh/back behaves predictably.
Prefer server‑side slicing for large datasets; ensure Table or List reflects the current page cleanly.
Use accessible labels and titles for buttons (Prev/Next) and indicate current page to assistive tech.
Consider infinite scroll for content feeds where paging is less meaningful.
Align placement near the data it controls and avoid duplicating controls unless justified.
Internationalize the built-in labels with the `labels` prop (instance-level) or a `ValetLocaleProvider` (app-wide). Resolution is instance `labels` > provider strings > built-in English.

examples.title: Controlled pagination
Visible window + autoFollow
